question: Implement a multilevel inheritance hierarchy: `Vehicle` (base) -> `Car` -> `SportsCar`. Each class should have a simple constructor that prints its name and a unique method (e.g., `startEngine()` in `Vehicle`, `drive()` in `Car`, `activateBoost()` in `SportsCar`).

test_cases.expected:
>> Initializing Base V_hicle Object! <<
>> Initializing Derived C_ar Object! <<
>> Initializing Super Derived S_portsCar Object! <<
V_hicle engine has been started up.
C_ar is now in motion, driving along.
S_portsCar's super boost feature has been activated!

errors: Inconsistent and poor code formatting, including indentation, makes the code hard to read and maintain.
Non-standard and verbose naming conventions (e.g., `V_hicle`, `_start_engine_method_`) reduce readability.
Excessive use of `std::endl` instead of `\n` forces a flush of the output buffer on every line, which can introduce minor performance overhead in applications with heavy console output.
